output: Implemented T1 in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/auto-scroll.ts`. `createAutoScroller` now accepts optional `onTick`, stores `lastClientX`/`lastClientY` in `update()`, and invokes `onTick` synchronously after scroll mutation and before scheduling the next RAF. Batch checkpoint file was not writable, so no checkpoint file was updated.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified in source that `update()` stores raw pointer coordinates before edge-distance calculations (`lastClientX`/`lastClientY` assigned at lines 31-34), and `tick()` invokes `onTick?.(lastClientX, lastClientY)` immediately after mutating `scrollLeft`/`scrollTop` and before `requestAnimationFrame(tick)` (`lines 25-28`). Checkpoint file was not writable, so progress was not mirrored there.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. `onTick` fires synchronously in `tick()` after scroll mutation and before the next RAF is requested, and `lastClientX`/`lastClientY` are updated at the start of `update()` before any edge-zone math so the stored coordinates remain the raw pointer position.
